candidate_problems.text: Operationalize 'selectivity' and 'transition suppression' in longitudinal behavioral or clinical data to distinguish grief from depression without relying on symptom checklists alone.
Determine when severe grief crosses from a localized loss-centered basin into a global depressive deformation, and whether there are early dynamical markers of that transition.
Test whether the state-space geometry differs across bereavement, melancholic depression, atypical depression, and trauma-related syndromes in ways predicted by the model.
Assess whether interventions should differ structurally—supporting oscillation and attachment processing in grief versus restoring global transition dynamics in depression.
